name: Krizhevsky et al., 'ImageNet Classification with Deep CNNs', NeurIPS 2012

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'AlexNet (Krizhevsky et al., 2012)'. TOP region (full width): an inline-SVG left-to-right pipeline of EIGHT equal-width stage boxes with right-pointing arrows. Each box carries THREE lines of dense text: layer name / kernel-stride / output volume: 'Conv1 | 11x11 s4 | 96@55x55', 'Conv2 | 5x5 pad2 | 256@27x27', 'Conv3 | 3x3 pad1 | 384@13x13', 'Conv4 | 3x3 pad1 | 384@13x13', 'Conv5 | 3x3 pad1 | 256@13x13', 'FC6 | - | 4096', 'FC7 | - | 4096', 'FC8 | - | 1000'. DIRECTLY BELOW the pipeline, an aligned row of EIGHT small parameter chips, one centered under each stage box and sharing the box widths: '35K', '307K', '885K', '664K', '442K', '37.7M', '16.8M', '4.1M'. BOTTOM region: TWO equal-width aligned panels - LEFT a dense bullet list ('ReLU -> faster training', 'Two GTX 580 GPUs (3GB), split across GPUs', 'Local response normalization', 'Overlapping max-pooling', 'Dropout 0.5 in FC layers', 'Augmentation: crops + PCA color jitter'); RIGHT a results table ('ILSVRC-2010 top-5 17.0%', 'ILSVRC-2012 top-5 15.3%', '60M params', '650K neurons', '~5 days, 2 GPUs'). Footnote at bottom.
requirements: All eight pipeline stage boxes are exactly equal width with a uniform gutter (>=16px) between adjacent boxes
The eight stage boxes share the same top edge and the same bottom edge (aligned row)
All THREE lines of text in every stage box are fully inside the box, none clipped or overflowing
Each of the eight parameter chips is centered under its stage box and aligned in a single row sharing one baseline
The parameter-chip row does not overlap the pipeline row or the bottom panels
Arrows connect adjacent stages and do not overlap any box text
The two bottom panels are equal width and share the same top and bottom edge
All six left bullets and all five right table rows are present and fully inside their panels
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge; every text element is at least 11px
task.paper: Krizhevsky et al., 'ImageNet Classification with Deep CNNs', NeurIPS 2012

ss_html:
AlexNet (Krizhevsky et al., 2012)

35K
307K
885K
664K
442K
37.7M
16.8M
4.1M

Key Innovations

  • ReLU → faster training
  • Two GTX 580 GPUs (3GB), split across GPUs
  • Local response normalization
  • Overlapping max-pooling
  • Dropout 0.5 in FC layers
  • Augmentation: crops + PCA color jitter

Results & Scale

ILSVRC-2010 top-517.0%
ILSVRC-2012 top-515.3%
Total parameters60M params
Neurons650K neurons
Training time~5 days, 2 GPUs
First CNN to win ImageNet, demonstrating the power of deep learning for computer vision tasks

ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks • Breakthrough that launched the deep learning revolution

trace.feedback:
Quality score: 30%
DOES NOT MEET REQUIREMENTS

Issues found:
  1. [CRITICAL] Pipeline stage boxes have unequal widths - Conv1 appears narrower than Conv2-Conv5, and FC6-FC8 appear wider than the convolutional layers (at top-center)
  2. [MAJOR] Parameter chips below pipeline boxes are not properly centered under their respective stage boxes - misalignment is visible across multiple stages (at top-center)
  3. [MAJOR] The two bottom panels (Architectural Innovations and Results & Scale) appear to have unequal widths, with the left panel being wider than the right panel (at bottom-center)
  4. [MINOR] Text in some pipeline boxes appears to be very close to the box edges, potentially at risk of clipping (at top-center)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Make all eight pipeline stage boxes exactly equal width with uniform gutters
  2. Center each parameter chip directly under its corresponding stage box
  3. Ensure both bottom panels have equal width and share aligned edges
  4. Add more padding inside pipeline boxes to prevent text from appearing too close to edges

trace.feedback:
Quality score: 35%
DOES NOT MEET REQUIREMENTS

Issues found:
  1. [CRITICAL] Pipeline stage boxes have unequal widths - Conv1 appears significantly narrower than Conv2-Conv5, and FC6-FC8 appear wider than the convolutional layers (RECURRED) (at top-center)
  2. [MAJOR] Parameter chips below pipeline boxes are not properly centered under their respective stage boxes - visible misalignment between chips and boxes above them (RECURRED) (at top-center)
  3. [MAJOR] The two bottom panels (Architectural Innovations and Results & Scale) have unequal widths, with the left panel appearing wider than the right panel (RECURRED) (at bottom)
  4. [MINOR] Text in pipeline boxes appears very close to box edges, particularly in Conv layers where multi-line text may be at risk of clipping (RECURRED) (at top-center)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Use CSS flexbox or grid with equal flex-grow values to ensure all 8 pipeline boxes have identical widths
  2. Calculate exact box widths mathematically: (total_width - 7_gutters) / 8 boxes, then apply fixed widths
  3. Use CSS text-align: center and equal container widths to properly center parameter chips under their boxes
  4. Apply identical width percentages (50% each) to both bottom panels with proper flex or grid layout
  5. Increase internal padding in pipeline boxes to prevent text from touching edges

AlexNet

Krizhevsky, Sutskever & Hinton, 2012 — Winner ILSVRC-2012
Architecture Pipeline — Input 227×227×3
Conv1 11×11, stride 4 96 @ 55×55 + MaxPool 3×3 s2
Conv2 5×5, pad=2 256 @ 27×27 + MaxPool 3×3 s2
Conv3 3×3, pad=1 384 @ 13×13 cross-GPU connect
Conv4 3×3, pad=1 384 @ 13×13 within GPU only
Conv5 3×3, pad=1 256 @ 13×13 + MaxPool 3×3 s2
FC6 fully connected 4096 units ReLU + Dropout 0.5
FC7 fully connected 4096 units ReLU + Dropout 0.5
FC8 (Output) fully connected 1000 classes Softmax output
